dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. The village is falling apart. People are saying there might be a way to stop this.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Sarah's been working on something at the laboratory. A cure. Last I heard, she had a sample but no way to finish the work.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Why hasn't she finished it?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Power's out at the lab. And the door's locked. Lab key went missing months ago — someone said it ended up out at the graveyard, of all places.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And the cure sample?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Hospital. Upper ward. Sarah left it there before things got bad. If you're really going to do this, you'll need George at the powerstation too — he's the only one who'll know what to do with a fuse.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anything else I should know?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Yes. Sarah went back to the lab to keep working. She's not well anymore. Be ready for that.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. I need the lab powered up.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Lab's on its own line. Needs a fuse. Don't have one here.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Where would I find one?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Last spare's inside the lab itself. Stupid place for it, but that's where it is.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: So I get inside, grab the fuse, bring it back here, and you walk me through it?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: No. You bring it back, I'll tell you which slot. You do it yourself. I'm not leaving the grid.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Easy. Identify yourself.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Just a survivor. Anne sent me. Sarah left a cure sample up here.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: She did. I've been sitting on it. Take it if you can use it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I can. Thanks, Marcus.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Don't waste it. That's all I ask.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah. It's me. Anne said you were here.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: You shouldn't be this close. The infection is progressing. I can still think — for now.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I have the cure sample. I'm going to get power back to this lab and synthesize it.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: The fuse is on the bench by the door. Take it. The synthesis machine will do the rest once power is restored. Hurry.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Hold on. I'll be back.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. Something on this island isn't adding up. Every road leads back to someone pulling strings.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: You've finally noticed. Took you long enough.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Viktor?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Viktor is the fist. But fists don't think. There's a man up at the outpost — calls himself the Doctor. He's the one feeding Viktor reasons to keep us all in line.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And you know this how?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Everyone passes through this store eventually. Viktor's men talk when they think I'm just stacking shelves. If you want to end this, you cut the head, not the hand.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'll need a way in. Outpost is locked.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Talk to George at the powerstation. He saw one of Viktor's men drop something near the cave a few weeks back. Could be your way in.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. Anne sent me. Said you saw something near the cave.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: One of Viktor's patrols. Dropped a key. Didn't go back for it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: An outpost key?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Brass. Outpost stamp. Still in the cave, last I checked.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: There's a doctor running things up there. I need to get to him.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Figured as much. Power keeps flickering — someone's running heavy equipment off the grid. Not us.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anything else I should know?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Cave's safe. Outpost won't be. Take wood and board yourself a fallback before you go in. Village has plenty.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: You walked into my house. Bold. Stupid, but bold.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'm not here for you, Viktor. I'm here for the man whispering in your ear.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: The Doctor? You think he runs this? He works for me.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then you won't mind if I have a word with him.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: Go on, then. See what your little conversation buys you. He's in the back. And when you're done, we'll have ours.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: Ah. The variable that wouldn't stay in its lane. Fascinating.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You started the outbreak. Didn't you.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: Started is such an imprecise word. I accelerated an inevitability. The island simply became... a controlled environment.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: People died. Are dying.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: People die regardless. My work survives. That is the only metric that matters.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Viktor knows what you really are?

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: Viktor believes he commands me. A useful misconception. He provides... infrastructure.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then I'm taking your infrastructure apart. Starting with your toolkit.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah. I found him. The one behind all of this.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Tell me.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: A doctor at the outpost. He engineered the outbreak. Calls it a controlled environment.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: That... actually clarifies several inconsistencies in my samples. The strain didn't behave like anything natural. It was designed.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Can you still finish the cure?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: If the strain was engineered, then it was meant to be reversible. Whoever built it kept a key. I'll find it. Knowing it's deliberate means I know what I'm looking for.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I have a toolkit now. We can start repairing what he broke — literally.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Then go. I'll work the science. You hold the rest of the island together.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah. I think it's time. There's a boat at the dock — Mary's boat. If we fix it, we can get people off the island.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: A boat. That's a finite solution to an infinite problem. But yes — getting survivors out is the priority now.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I've got a toolkit. I'll need wood. Mary will know what else.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: There's lumber stacked in the village square. I saw it on the way in. Take it. And… be careful out there. Viktor's people are watching the roads.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Noted. I'll go talk to Mary first — see what she actually needs.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Good. One step at a time. That's how this gets done.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Mary. How bad is the boat?

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Hull's split along the port side. Nothing fancy — needs timber and a decent toolkit. You bring both, she floats.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I've got the toolkit. Wood's in the village square.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Then stop talking and go fetch it. Tide turns in a few hours and I'm not waiting on this rock another night.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood. And once she's fixed — you'll take whoever wants out?

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Anyone breathing and not biting. Bring them. I'll make room.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. We're getting Mary's boat seaworthy. Taking anyone who wants out.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Took you long enough to come to that conclusion. Half the village has been waiting for someone to say it out loud.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Spread the word quietly. Anyone wants out, they should head to the dock.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Quietly is the only way these days. Viktor's been twitchy. Watch yourself near the outpost road.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'll keep my distance. Linda at the farm — think she'll come?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Linda's stubborn but she's not stupid. Tell her the boat's real and she might surprise you. Take her some good news — and maybe some of her own food back. She'll appreciate the gesture.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Linda. Mary's boat is being repaired. We're leaving the island.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Mm. Heard the engine cough last week. Figured she'd try.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: There's room for you. And we could use what food you can spare — for the crossing.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Food I've got. Crowds I don't care for. But a boat is better than waiting for the wind to change.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then come to the dock when you're ready. I'll take the supplies now.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Take them. Sack by the gate. Don't drop it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. We're leaving. Mary's boat. Tonight.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Good. Grid won't last the month anyway.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Come with us.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: I will. Just need to kill the main breaker so this place doesn't burn behind us. Five minutes.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Meet us at the dock. Don't be late.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: I'm never late.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Got the wood. Got the toolkit. Got food for the crossing.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Then quit posing on my dock and patch the hull. I'll get the engine warm.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Others are coming. Anne, Linda, George. Maybe Sarah.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Good. The more hands, the better the watch. Now — work.
